title: Beau Is Afraid
logline: An extremely anxious man's journey to visit his mother becomes a surreal odyssey through urban chaos, suburban dysfunction, forest communes, and psychological confrontation with his deepest fears and maternal trauma.
antagonistOrConflict: Beau's crippling anxiety and his manipulative, controlling mother

actBreaks.description: Beau is rescued from his urban nightmare and taken in by Grace and Roger's suburban family.

validation.ruling: Scene 35 ends Act 1 as the corrupt policeman drives Beau away from the police station into unknown territory, fundamentally changing the story direction from urban apartment crisis to mysterious kidnapping scenario.

actBreaks.description: Beau flees into the forest after Toni's apparent death, leaving the suburban world behind.

validation.ruling: Scene 68 ends Act 2 as Beau flees into the forest after being accused of murder, representing an irreversible shift from suburban family dynamics to wilderness survival and forcing him into a completely new environment.

actBreaks.description: Beau finally arrives at his mother's house in Castlestone, beginning the final confrontation.

validation.ruling: Scene 96 ends Act 3 as Beau arrives in his hometown Castlestone, marking the transition from forest survival back to his origins and setting up the final confrontation with his past.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a triptych of anxiety-driven episodes, each representing a different stage of Beau's psychological journey. The urban section establishes his baseline terror and inability to function in the world. The suburban section offers false comfort that turns toxic, revealing how even well-meaning environments can become prisons for the anxious mind. The forest commune section strips away civilization entirely, forcing Beau into primal survival mode. Finally, the maternal confrontation brings all themes to a head in a surreal, nightmarish climax that blends reality with psychological projection.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing accelerates through each section - the urban opening is deliberately slow and claustrophobic (scenes 1-35), the suburban middle builds tension through domestic dysfunction (scenes 36-67), the forest section moves at breakneck speed with constant threat (scenes 68-95), and the final maternal confrontation is a sustained psychological assault (scenes 96-119). Each section has its own rhythm that mirrors Beau's mental state.

incitingIncident.description: Beau calls his landlord unsuccessfully, then calls his mother who pressures him to come despite his situation and threatens emotional consequences if he doesn't.

validation.ruling: Scene 17 shatters Beau's ordinary world when his keys are stolen, forcing him to call his mother and reveal he'll miss his flight, which triggers her suspicious interrogation and fundamentally changes his situation from routine travel preparation to crisis management.

midpoint.description: Grace opens the garage door to find Toni dead with blue coating on her mouth while Beau stands over her, then Grace screams and desperately tries to revive Toni.

validation.ruling: Scene 67 serves as the midpoint where Beau is discovered standing over Toni's dead body, accused of murder by Grace, creating a point of no return that shifts him from passive victim to active fugitive and dramatically raises the stakes.

climax.description: Beau collapses hysterically in the hallway as his mother reveals that the monstrous figure is his father, while the therapist slaps him and laughs at his distress.

validation.ruling: Scene 112 is setup rather than confrontation - the climax occurs in Scene 114 where Beau faces the ultimate revelation that the monstrous figure is his father, representing the peak psychological confrontation and resolution of his deepest fears about his identity and family.

scenes.heading: INT. WAITING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau sits anxiously in a therapist's waiting room, looking at a disturbing dental magazine while a surveillance camera watches him.

scenes.heading: INT. THERAPIST’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau tells his therapist about accidentally drinking mouthwash and expresses fear about visiting his mother for his father's death anniversary.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUILDING - EVNING
scenes.summary: Beau exits the building and encounters a crowd filming a man on a roof, with someone telling him they're trying to get the man to jump.

scenes.heading: INT. PHARMACY - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: At the pharmacy, Beau tries to warn the pharmacist about junkies stealing drugs, but the pharmacist ignores him and the theft continues.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARK - EVENING
scenes.summary: Beau peacefully feeds bread to ducks at a park lake.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBWAY - EVENING
scenes.summary: On the subway, Beau watches a father and son with longing, then becomes uncomfortable when a woman stares at him lustfully.

scenes.heading: EXT. APARTMENT BUILDING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau hurriedly enters his rundown apartment building surrounded by homeless people, rushing to avoid contact with them.

scenes.heading: INT. ELEVATOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau enters the malfunctioning elevator where all buttons light up when he presses one for the third floor.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Sandy tells Beau that their neighbor Flavio was bitten by a Brown Recluse spider and had a cardiac arrest.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S APARTMENT - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Beau enters his dark apartment where a Brown Recluse spider retreats under the couch unnoticed as he turns on the lights.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Beau looks at a yearbook photo of himself with a girl named Elaine, then packs while watching news about a celebrity murder.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - LATER THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau tries to sleep but is kept awake by city noise and loud music from next door, plugging his ears with his fingers.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Beau wakes up at 3:02 PM to his alarm clock and panics about being late for his flight.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Beau frantically brushes his teeth while packing his toiletries.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Beau grabs his plane ticket, rushes out leaving his key in the door and suitcase in the hall, then runs back for floss.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau discovers his key and suitcase have been stolen from the hallway, and a small man tells him he's fucked.
scenes.turningPointNote: The theft of Beau's keys sets off the chain of events that will prevent him from reaching his mother

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S APARTMENT - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Beau calls his landlord unsuccessfully, then calls his mother who pressures him to come despite his situation and threatens emotional consequences if he doesn't.

scenes.heading: INT. BUILDING LOBBY - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Beau props open the building door with a phone book and stares out at the homeless people blocking his path to the store.

scenes.heading: EXT. APARTMENT BUILDING - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau sprints through the crowd of homeless people and enters the convenience store.

scenes.heading: INT. CONVENIENCE STORE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau's credit card is declined at the store, and while he searches for change, a bum discovers and removes his door prop.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau gets locked out of his building as homeless people enter and take over his apartment, and the store cashier refuses to help him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau loses access to his home and watches helplessly as it's invaded

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - LATER THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau climbs a tree to watch homeless people having sex and cooking a skunk in his apartment through the night.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - NEXT MORNING
scenes.summary: Beau wakes up in the tree, sees his apartment appears empty, and enters through the smashed front door of the building.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - MORNING
scenes.summary: Beau finds a dead homeless man at his apartment entrance with the Brown Recluse spider resting on the corpse's chest.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S APARTMENT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Beau tries to kill the Brown Recluse with his TV but fails, the spider escapes, and he locks himself inside after moving the corpse.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau surveys his destroyed kitchen.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau checks his bedroom which shows evidence of drug abuse, sex, and violence, finding the closets empty.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau thoroughly checks his bathroom to ensure it's empty and safe.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Beau calls a locksmith who schedules an appointment for 4:30 PM.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Beau tries to book a new flight and call his mother but gets a wrong number, with a young man answering instead of his mother.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau wakes up on his living room floor calling out for his mom.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau looks through a photo album of memories with his mother, focusing on a childhood photo of them embracing.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau takes a bath while listening to soothing music, blaming himself for not being there for his mother, when he hears a squeaking sound above.

scenes.heading: EXT. APARTMENT BUILDING - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau runs naked and screaming from his building to a policeman, desperately trying to explain his situation while the officer draws his weapon.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The policeman drives Beau past the police station while taking his photo and listening to radio reports about violent crimes, refusing to answer Beau's questions.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau is taken by a corrupt cop away from safety, leading to his stabbing and rescue by Grace's family

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - NIGHT/CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau wakes up bandaged in a bright bedroom with an IV, and Grace explains he's been sleeping for two days and is stable.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - THE NEXT MORNING
scenes.summary: Grace and Roger sit at Beau's bedside in the morning, and Beau mentions his mother used to watch him sleep.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Beau explains to Roger and Grace over breakfast how he got stabbed, mentioning he was supposed to visit his mother for his father's anniversary.

scenes.heading: INT. STUDY - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau calls his mother's attorney who confirms his mother is dead and reveals she died of a heart attack after learning Beau missed his flight.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau learns his mother is dead and that his absence may have caused her fatal heart attack

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Beau and Grace work on a puzzle while Roger reads, and their hostile daughter Toni arrives home asking if her room is off-limits again.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAWN - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau calls his therapist about his mother's death and discusses whether he needs to go home, with the therapist questioning his motivations.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Grace sets up a therapy appointment for Beau with her friend Judy who specializes in trauma treatment.

scenes.heading: INT. TONI’S BEDROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Toni watches Beau crying on the couch and tells someone on the phone about him while taking her mother's prescription pills.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR (MOVING) - DAY
scenes.summary: Grace drives Beau to therapy and they discuss his high school crush Elaine during the car ride.

scenes.heading: INT. BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: Grace leads Beau through a building lobby past Jeeves, a traumatized former POW security guard.

scenes.heading: INT. WAITING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau meets therapist Judy in the waiting room and she introduces herself warmly.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Judy explains the sympathetic and parasympathetic nervous systems to Beau using a diagram on a dry erase board.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR (MOVING) - DAY
scenes.summary: Grace tells Beau that Judy liked him and wants to see him twice a week, and Beau agrees he'd feel good about that.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Toni confronts Beau at home, questioning his story and revealing he's not the first charity case her parents have brought home.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR (MOVING) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Toni drives Beau and her friend Liz, who pressures Beau to smoke a joint, and when he refuses and suggests going back, Toni slams on the brakes.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau sits stoned on a log watching Liz wrap her mouth around a car's tailpipe while Toni revs the engine, then they switch positions.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR (MOVING) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Toni, Beau and Liz drive back from the desert in silence.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Toni and Beau enter the dark house where Roger and Grace are asleep, and Toni asks Beau what he's waiting for.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Toni aggressively insists Beau take her bed despite his protests, then tucks him in forcefully while he remains uncomfortable.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - MORNING
scenes.summary: At breakfast, Grace asks if they had a good time and Beau reluctantly says yes after Toni deflects to him.

scenes.heading: INT. BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: Grace walks Beau through the building lobby past Jeeves, whose eyes follow them to the elevator.

scenes.heading: INT. JUDY’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Judy guides Beau through a body relaxation exercise where he identifies tension in his neck, shoulders, back and teeth.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR (MOVING) - EVENING
scenes.summary: Beau tells Grace he wants to go to Castlestone for his mother, and she reassures him she's not mad about leaving.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau tries to sleep on the couch but Toni angrily insists he must sleep in her bed per her parents' rules.

scenes.heading: INT. GRACE’S & ROGER’S BEDROOM - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Toni wakes Roger and Grace to complain about Beau sleeping on the couch, but Roger threatens to get cross when she calls them hypocritical.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Toni whispers a threat to Beau that she'll hold rubber cement under his nose while he sleeps, then storms off.

scenes.heading: INT. TONI’S BEDROOM = SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Toni slams her bedroom door and curls up angrily in her sleeping bag, cursing Beau for coming into her home.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau lies gripping his blanket while hearing Toni's muffled swearing and anticipating consequences of her hatred.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: Beau practices feeling his body sensations until Roger pops in to tell him it's grill time, then Toni appears as he puts on shoes.

scenes.heading: INT. GARAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Toni drags Beau to the garage and demands he get high with her, accusing him of stealing her parents and claiming his mother wasn't murdered.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Grace hears commotion in the garage including Beau pleading with Toni to stop, then rushes toward the garage.

scenes.heading: INT. GARAGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Grace opens the garage door to find Toni dead with blue coating on her mouth while Beau stands over her, then Grace screams and desperately tries to revive Toni.
scenes.turningPointNote: Toni's death fundamentally changes Beau's situation and forces him to flee

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau runs frantically into the forest while hyperventilating and unable to stop.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOUSE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Roger's anguished wailing echoes from the house as he discovers his daughter, startling birds from the trees.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Beau continues sprinting through the woods crying hysterically until he crashes face-first into a tree branch and falls unconscious, then has a memory of promising his mother they'd never leave each other.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - EVENING
scenes.summary: Beau wakes up in the forest at sunset, drinks from a filthy pond full of dead fish, then sees a frightened man in camouflage.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Michael leads Beau to a treehouse colony called Orphans of the Forest where a confident naked man greets Beau with an aggressive hug and introduces himself as Brennan.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau enters a new world/community that will dominate the next act

scenes.heading: INT. TENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau lies in his sleeping bag in the tent staring at the ceiling and tells himself his mother was murdered.

scenes.heading: EXT. DINING SPACE - THE NEXT MORNING
scenes.summary: Twenty Orphans sit in silent meditation at a feast until Brennan begins serving himself, then Billy tells Beau he can eat now.

scenes.heading: EXT. TREEHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau watches the Orphans work while Brennan oversees and offers him a task.

scenes.heading: EXT. GARDEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau pulls weeds with Sunflower and experiences deja vu when Brennan tells him to feel his feet, causing the image to quake.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Jeeves marches down a street in hunting gear carrying a photo of Beau with his face circled in red, then turns toward the forest as thunder strikes.

scenes.heading: INT. TONI’S BEDROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Grace sits weeping on Toni's bed holding her photo, blaming herself for bringing Beau into their home, then cracks the glass and calls for Jeeves to rip Beau apart.

scenes.heading: INT. VITAMIN SHACK - DAY
scenes.summary: Brennan leads Beau to receive a vitamin injection from a naked Aboriginal witch doctor despite Beau's reluctance about the unknown serum.

scenes.heading: INT. GIANT TENT - DAY
scenes.summary: While changing into meditation robes, a bearded Orphan notices and comments on Beau's unusually large testicles.

scenes.heading: INT. MEDITATION SHACK - DAY
scenes.summary: Brennan guides an anxious Beau into meditation position, dismissing Beau's attempts to discuss leaving for Castlestone and his mother's death.

scenes.heading: INT. YESEKOV’S SHACK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Orphans gather around dying Yesekov's bed in reverent silence while Billy explains to Beau that death is beautiful and the body is a prison.

scenes.heading: INT. MEDITATION SHACK - EVENING
scenes.summary: During evening meditation, everyone sits too disturbed by recent events to properly meditate, with some keeping their eyes open skeptically.

scenes.heading: EXT. DINING SPACE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At dinner, Beau eyes an escape route while Brennan distributes mail and claims Yesekov's spirit visited him with instructions for a special ceremony.

scenes.heading: INT. MAIN TREEHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Brennan puts Beau under a spotlight on a couch and interrogates him with a strange machine, extracting details about his relationship with his mother and his sexual inexperience.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jeeves marches through the forest with nightmare-like determination, faces off with a grizzly bear, then rides the bear up a hill.

scenes.heading: INT. GIANT TENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau lies awake while an Orphan throws knives at a photo of George Clooney, claiming the actor pushed his father in front of a train.

scenes.heading: INT. GIANT TENT - NGIHT
scenes.summary: Brennan wakes Charles and leads him outside while Beau notices Billy's map and pockets it.

scenes.heading: EXT. GIANT TENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau follows Charles and Brennan outside to discover the other Orphans in ritual face-paint around a fire with an older man tied to a tree.

scenes.heading: INT. GIANT TENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau lies extremely awake battling a panic attack, then tries to leave for the bathroom but Billy questions why he won't use the hole in the tent.

scenes.heading: EXT. GIANT TENT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau tries to escape but an alarm sounds and Brennan confronts him with a spotlight, accusing him of being sealed shut and ugly.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau runs through the woods with unprecedented ferocity, his legs moving as fast as his addled brain.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: After running all night, an exhausted Beau reaches a pond and checks Billy's map to discover the nearest town is only one mile away.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Beau emerges from bushes onto a road, finds a bus stop with a map showing Castlestone as a destination, and boards an approaching bus.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau finally escapes the forest community and heads toward his ultimate destination

scenes.heading: INT. BUS (MOVING) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau sits in the back of a mostly empty bus driving quietly through the night.

scenes.heading: EXT. CASTLESTONE STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: A haggard Beau exits the bus in Castlestone and walks zombie-like through the street while residents recognize and stare at him.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEAU’S MOTHER’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau approaches his mother's house, touching the mailbox sentimentally before looking up the driveway at the large, beautiful beach house.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S MOTHER’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau enters his mother's house and walks past a standee of his mother advertising antidepressants and various framed pharmaceutical posters.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau finally reaches his mother's house, the destination he's been trying to reach throughout the story

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau walks through a hallway lined with photos documenting his childhood with his mother, including homeschooling, cooking, and a bee attack incident.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S MOTHER’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau enters his mother's bedroom, sits on her bed overwhelmed, smells a strand of her hair from the pillow, then pulls a journal from her shelves and begins reading.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S MOTHER’S HOUSE - DAY - FLASHBACK
scenes.summary: In flashback, young Beau's mother writes in her journal while 8-year-old Beau draws on the floor, as she reflects in voiceover about lying to him that his father died during conception.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S MOTHER’S BEDROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Beau closes his mother's journal, stands up in confused rage after reading the truth, and discovers a door marked 'MAMA'S WORKSHOP.'

scenes.heading: INT. MAMA’S WORKSHOP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau enters the workshop filled with advertising materials and discovers a pitch board for 'MAMA WILMINGTON'S ULTIMATE SURVEILLANCE' featuring his mother's claim that she observed her own son for 23 years without detection.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau discovers his mother has been surveilling him his entire life

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau scrubs himself vigorously in the shower and then shaves angrily in front of the mirror.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau searches through a phonebook and calls Elaine Bray, speaking to her mother who tells him Elaine is probably at a bar.

scenes.heading: INT. SQUIRREL DEN BAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau enters a seedy biker bar, orders a drink, spots Elaine at the bar, and nervously approaches her to introduce himself as a high school friend.

scenes.heading: INT. ELAINE’S CAR (MOVING) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elaine drives Beau while touching his thigh, veers into the median, and asks where to turn for his place as he grows increasingly uncomfortable.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S MOTHER’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau and Elaine enter his mother's house where she makes suggestive comments about keeping clothes on and asks for candles.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S MOTHER’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elaine lights candles in Beau's mother's bedroom and tells Beau to go to the bathroom so she can get ready.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Beau stares at himself in the bathroom mirror and gives himself a pep talk, declaring that he's a man.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Beau and Elaine have sex in his mother's bed, with Beau extremely nervous and tense throughout the encounter, and Elaine putting a condom on him before guiding him into her.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau loses his virginity, fulfilling his mother's prophecy about death during sex

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau comes downstairs with car keys to find his supposedly dead mother alive in a chair, while two muscular men carry Elaine's corpse down the stairs and outside.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau's mother reveals she's alive and Elaine is dead, confirming the family curse

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET PUNISHMENT ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau is locked in a dark punishment room filled with old furniture and toys, where he hears raspy breathing and sees a giant, bulky figure of trembling flesh standing fifteen feet tall.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau collapses hysterically in the hallway as his mother reveals that the monstrous figure is his father, while the therapist slaps him and laughs at his distress.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau discovers his father is alive but transformed into a monstrous being

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau walks numbly down the stairs past the bullet-ridden bodies of his mother's two young assistants lying in a pool of blood.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau enters the kitchen, finds his mother's keys and his own stolen keys on the counter, and hears a fire igniting upstairs.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEAU’S MOM’S HOUSE - NIGHT/MORNING
scenes.summary: Beau leaves the burning house with a gun, finds the small man in a parka now dressed as a chauffeur sleeping in his mother's car, drops the keys, and walks to a lake where he gets in a sailing boat.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau abandons his mother's burning house and chooses to escape by boat

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Beau sits in the sailing boat as it floats toward a fork with two tunnels marked 'TOWN' and '???' and steers toward the unknown tunnel.

scenes.heading: EXT. OCEAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau's boat floats into the open ocean surrounded by placid blue water, and he discovers supplies including water, food, binoculars, fishing rod, bird watching book, and War & Peace under a tarp.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau reaches the ocean with provisions, suggesting a new chapter or ending to his journey
